welcome.title: Welcome to Ordinay
welcome.subtitle: Your legal practice, organized.
welcome.description: Ordinay helps you manage your clients, lawsuits, tasks, and finances — all in one place. Let's take a quick tour to get you started.
welcome.skipNote: You can always replay this from Settings.

dashboard.title: Your Control Center
dashboard.description: This is your dashboard — the place you'll start each day.

highlights.stats: See your key numbers at a glance: clients, active lawsuits, pending tasks, and revenue.
highlights.upcoming: Upcoming events and deadlines appear here. No need to check multiple places.
highlights.quick: Need to create something fast? Quick actions are always within reach.

dashboard.tip: You don't need to open everything. Ordinay surfaces what matters.

workflow.title: How Ordinay Works
workflow.subtitle: Understanding the flow
workflow.intro: Ordinay is built around a simple hierarchy. Once you understand it, everything clicks.

clients.title: Start with Clients
clients.description: Every piece of work begins with a client. Add your clients first — they're the foundation.
clients.detail: Each client can have multiple legal matters. Think of clients as the containers for everything else.

dossiers.title: Dossiers: Your Legal Matters
dossiers.description: A dossier represents a legal file. It belongs to a client and holds all related work.
dossiers.detail: Example: "Divorce Proceedings - Smith" or "Contract Dispute - Acme Corp". Each dossier tracks its own tasks, deadlines, and history.

tasks.title: Tasks Keep You on Track
tasks.description: Tasks are the actions you need to complete. They live inside dossiers or stand alone for personal work.
tasks.detail: Set due dates, mark priorities, and check them off as you go. Nothing slips through the cracks.

missions.title: Missions & Hearings
missions.description: Missions are scheduled events: court hearings, client meetings, expert consultations.
missions.detail: They appear in your dashboard's upcoming events. You'll never miss an important date.

workflow.summary: Clients → Dossiers → Tasks & Missions. That's the mental model. Simple.

financial.title: Stay in Control of Your Finances
financial.description: Ordinay tracks your legal fees and expenses. Here's what you need to know.

points.fees: Fees are what you earn from clients. Attach them to dossiers to track payments.
points.expenses: Expenses are costs you incur: court fees, travel, expert consultations.
points.tracking: See what's paid, what's pending, and what's overdue — at a glance.

financial.reassurance: Nothing is sent automatically. Nothing syncs without your permission. You stay in control.
financial.tip: Don't worry about mastering this now. Start simple, add detail as you go.

preferences.title: Make It Yours
preferences.description: A few quick settings to personalize your experience.

language.title: Language
language.description: Choose your preferred language. You can change this anytime in Settings.

theme.title: Theme
theme.description: Light or dark? Pick what's comfortable for your eyes.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Get reminders for upcoming deadlines and events.

preferences.skipNote: All of these can be changed later in Settings. No pressure.

completion.title: You're Ready!
completion.description: That's all you need to know to get started.
completion.encouragement: Explore freely — you can't break anything. Every action is reversible, and your data is safe.

tips.title: Quick reminders
tips.tip2: Create a dossier for their legal matter
tips.tip3: Add tasks to track your work

completion.replay: Need a refresher? You can replay this tour anytime from Settings.

guidedTutorial.title: Want a hands-on walkthrough?
guidedTutorial.description: Take an interactive guided tour where we'll highlight the interface and walk you through creating your first client.

settings.replayDescription: Take the guided tour again to refresh your memory.
settings.tipsDescription: Display helpful hints as you navigate the app.

tooltips.pressEsc: Press Esc to exit at any time
